golden_categories.name: Basic URL Validation
golden_categories.description: Covers general URL validation with standard protocols and formats.

golden_categories.name: Localhost and Non-TLD URLs
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs without protocols or with localhost and non-TLD domains.

golden_categories.name: Protocol Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests related to handling protocols, including custom protocols and protocol validation.

golden_categories.name: Underscore Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with underscores in the hostname.

golden_categories.name: TLD and Trailing Dot Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for URLs without TLDs and with trailing dots.

golden_categories.name: Protocol Relative URLs
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling protocol-relative URLs and related options.

golden_categories.name: Fragment and Query Component Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling fragments and query components in URLs.

golden_categories.name: Authentication Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling authentication information in URLs.

golden_categories.name: Host Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating hostnames, including whitelists, blacklists, and regular expressions.

golden_categories.name: Security and Vulnerability Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling security-related cases, such as rejecting invalid protocols and parsing differences.

golden_categories.name: Length Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URL length and configuring maximum allowed length.

generated_categories.name: Protocol Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests related to protocol requirements, custom protocols, and protocol-relative URLs.

generated_categories.name: Host and Domain Validation

generated_categories.name: Fragment and Query Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests focusing on the handling of fragments and query components in URLs.

generated_categories.name: Authentication Handling

generated_categories.name: Length Validation

generated_categories.name: Empty URL Validation

generated_categories.name: URL Structure and Parsing
generated_categories.description: Tests validating URL structure, including invalid split elements and malformed authentication formats.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on basic URL validation with standard protocols and formats.
GENC-1 covers basic URL validation, which partially overlaps with GC-2's focus on localhost and non-TLD URLs.
Both categories focus on protocol handling, including custom protocols and protocol-relative URLs.
Both categories focus on validating URLs with underscores in the hostname.
GENC-4 includes tests for trailing dots, which partially overlaps with GC-5's focus on TLD and trailing dot handling.
GENC-2 includes tests for protocol-relative URLs, which partially overlaps with GC-6's focus.
Both categories focus on handling fragments and query components in URLs.
Both categories focus on handling authentication information in URLs.
Both categories focus on host validation, including whitelists, blacklists, and regular expressions.
GENC-2 includes protocol validation, which partially overlaps with GC-10's focus on security and vulnerability handling.
Both categories focus on URL length validation and configuration.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 covers basic URL validation, which strongly matches GC-1 and partially overlaps with GC-2's focus on localhost and non-TLD URLs.
GENC-2 covers protocol handling, which strongly matches GC-3 and partially overlaps with GC-6 and GC-10.
GENC-3 focuses on validating URLs with underscores, which strongly matches GC-4.
GENC-4 includes host validation and trailing dots, which partially overlaps with GC-5 and strongly matches GC-9.
GENC-5 focuses on handling fragments and query components, which strongly matches GC-7.
GENC-6 focuses on authentication handling, which strongly matches GC-8.
GENC-7 focuses on URL length validation, which strongly matches GC-11.
GENC-8 focuses on port parsing, which partially overlaps with GC-1's basic URL validation.
GENC-9 focuses on empty URL validation, which does not map to any golden category.
GENC-10 focuses on URL structure and parsing, which does not map to any golden category.

novel_generated_categories.reason: GENC-9 introduces a new focus on empty URL validation, which is not covered by any golden category.
GENC-10 introduces a new focus on URL structure and parsing, which is not covered by any golden category.
